bullets.text: Q3 CR 92.9 — on par with prior year; YTD CR tracking well within targets; net income comparable to prior year after adjusting for favorable prior-year tax item; all products and channels meeting profitability targets.

Written premiums grew 5% in Q3; YTD growth 6%; Personal Lines alone grew ~6% in Q3; Commercial Auto PIFs still down 1% YOY — needs large-state recovery (particularly Florida) to return to positive territory.

Agency auto PIF +3% YOY; Direct auto PIF +14% YOY; policy life expectancy up notably for both channels — highest levels; shopping activity appeared elevated in Q3 2009 creating a moderating comparison base for Q3 2010.

$223M hybrid debt retired in Q3 at 95 cents on dollar; 10.7M shares repurchased YTD; $1 extraordinary dividend declared early Q4; Gainshare tracking ~1.65 factor — on pace for largest-ever variable dividend; portfolio total return 3.3% Q3, 6.5% YTD.

Bodily injury severity continuing to decline — unusual direction not easily reconciled with general experience; frequency manageable; PIP states improving results and attention shifting toward restoring growth; no significant hurricane losses in Q3.

J.D. Power National Auto Insurance Study ranking improved for 7th consecutive year; named top-rated overall carrier by independent agents in J.D. Power Agency Satisfaction Study; Jeff Charney hired as Chief Marketing Officer from Aflac.

Pricing trends modest; PIP states continuing improved results; focus shifting from remediation to growth restoration in challenged states; ongoing state-by-state rate review maintained as operational foundation.

Investment portfolio positioned near low end of duration guidelines — preference for credit/reinvestment risk over interest rate risk; pretax recurring yield 3.6% for the year; portfolio slightly overweight preferred stocks in financial sector being reduced.

Increasing conversion and retention driving results as new business growth moderates; both Agency and Direct retention improving; multi-product household initiative building toward 1M customers with multiple Progressive products.

Snapshot repackage on track — early rollout and transition from prior versions meeting redesign objectives; consumer experience simplified; 2011 national rollout with active marketing campaign planned; Name Your Price contributing to Direct growth.
